summary:
The v7.0 shipped doc package (6 files in /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5/docs/v7/) documents a LOCKED, demo-ready book: "V7.0 = the V6 core-7 book (7 equal-capital sleeves, 1/N, IC Markets EU Raw, €10k) with the calendar-quarterly re-split replaced by the concentration-band trigger BAND_SYM_25, plus the H9 delta-resize on each re-split." Nothing else changed vs V6 — same 7 instruments, same sleeve params, same inverse-vol sizing. The band rule: once per UTC day at 00:00 rollover compute each slot's share of total book equity (realized balance + floating P&L per slot / Σ slots); re-split the WHOLE book to equal capital if any slot's share > 0.25 (InpBandUp) or < (1/N)/1.75 = 0.0816 at N=7 (InpBandDownDiv=1.75); minimum 5 days between re-splits (InpBandMinGapDays=5, "load-bearing... must be implemented exactly"). The 3 S6 legs are summed into ONE slot before the share test (7 slots, floor computed at runtime from enabled-sleeve count, NOT hardcoded). A k=2.5 harvest backstop (InpHarvestK=2.5, re-seed any slot >2.5× its window-start seed) is retained but subsumed (0/31 fires in Python, 5 legitimate fires in MT5, 4 of them inside the 5-day band min-gap). At each re-split, same-sign sleeves ADD/REDUCE to the new equal-capital target (H9 delta-resize) instead of close-all+reopen; reversals still close+reopen; 266/269 = 98.9% of held-and-active resizes used the delta path. Live leverage is R=10 (InpRisk=10), chosen from the MT5 real-tick R-frontier (reports _32–_37) as the Sharpe peak (2.03) with the lowest eq-DD of the high-R points; R12 is the CAGR knee.

Two engines produce the numbers and the docs are scrupulous about attribution. (1) MT5 real-tick (PortfolioV7.mq5, IC-native costs, 2020-01→2026-06 crash-inclusive, R10): CAGR 96.1%, Maximal eq-DD (€-worst) 20.9%, Relative eq-DD (%-worst / crisis tail, the Mar-2020 COVID event €10.6k→€6.9k) 35.6%, Sharpe 2.03, PF 1.67, min ML 190%, history quality 84%, volume_rejects 0, losing quarters 3/24 (worst 2020Q1 −21.8%), negative years 0/6. The €782,092 figure appears ONLY in DASHBOARD.html (hero equity, generated by mt5/reconcile/v8/build_dashboard.py), labeled "€10,000 → ... R10 · MT5 real-tick, worst-mark accounting"; the math (78.2× ≈ 95.5% CAGR over 6.5y) matches the full 2020-01→2026-06 MT5 R10 window even though the hero subtitle loosely says "over 2020–2025". (2) Python band book (READ-ONLY gbandrebal/sim.run_generic = v51_bandharvest._run_window, idealized close-and-reseed, R8, IC 2020-25): CAGR 89.7% (anchor 89.72%), MaxDD 15.70% bd close-mark / 19.44% tick-floating (the honest, stricter mark), Sharpe 2.58, final equity €532,230, 31 band triggers. The full 6-tier validation battery (7/7 hard gates) ran on the R8 Python book; R10 is the live leverage from the MT5 frontier. At matched R8 the MT5 tester retains ~96% of Python CAGR (86.5/89.7) vs V6's ~65–73% — explicitly flagged "observed, not guaranteed". Reconciliation verdict is `reconciled_with_notes`: 4561 rows compared, 85.7% exact <1e-3, 292/300 capped/deterministic sleeve targets byte-exact, 99.4% direction agreement, all 650 residuals attributed to 5 benign classes, 0 unexplained logic mismatches; band rule exact (0 dead-zone fires) with the EA firing ~2× the idealized Python count (53 vs 27 over 2020-25; 57 incl. 2026H1) by design, because real floating P&L carried across delta-resizes makes shares drift more.

Documentation style is a strong house standard worth replicating: every doc opens with an H1 "V7.0 <topic>" plus a bold one-paragraph definition naming the code source of truth (EA path + preset + Python engine) and cross-linking sibling docs via relative markdown links; every doc states "all numbers are in-sample (IC 2020-25 + 2026-H1 real-tick); there is no post-2026 holdout — the live demo is the falsification test" both near the top AND at the bottom; heavy use of pipe tables for every numeric claim; bold key numbers inline; explicitly labeled "Honest caveats" / "Honest scope" / "Honest notes (none is a defect)" / "Engine honesty" sections that disclose weaknesses rather than bury them (R5 anomaly, band-width sensitivity, negQ recount from 0 to 3, sizing-mechanism dependence); a scorecard table (Tier | Test | Result | Gate | Verdict with ✅/⏳ emoji) in VALIDATION.md; a checkbox sign-off split into DONE [x] / OPEN [ ]; RECONCILIATION.md leads with the verdict token in bold on line 1 and ends with "Bottom line"; DEMO.md uses a dated "Where we stand (2026-07-09)", numbered deployment steps, a Watch/Expected-fingerprint/Red-flag monitoring table, numbered decision rules, and a "Definition of done". One internal contradiction: VALIDATION.md's header and sign-off are stale (list the MT5 R-sweep and EA reconciliation as OPEN and quote the old ~65% retention), while STRATEGY.md, PERFORMANCE.md, RECONCILIATION.md and DEMO.md (updated later) declare both DONE and quote ~96% retention.
key_facts: V7.0 definition (STRATEGY.md:17-27): V6 core-7 book + BAND_SYM_25 concentration-band re-split + H9 delta-resize; nothing else changed. Design philosophy: multi-sleeve regime-diverse, equal-weight 1/N, always-on, self-gating, NO market-timing/regime-switching overlay (de-risking backfires on a convergent book); leverage R is the single mechanical knob.

The 7 sleeves (STRATEGY.md:57-65, % of net P&L): BOOK_XAU XAUUSD Donchian breakout + overnight value-area ~43%; BOOK_USTEC USTEC(NASDAQ-100) 200-MA equity regime + Monday effect ~20%; S5_JPY USDJPY rate-divergence carry trend-gated ~10%; S1_ETH ETHUSD crypto momentum (regime + fast/slow) ~9%; ZC_EG EURGBP multi-window z-score mean-reversion (only long/short sleeve) ~8%; S6_OPEXUSD 3-leg USD opex basket (UJ/AU/NZ, ~1/21 each) ~4%; BTC_REP BTCUSD financing-hurdle momentum (−20%/yr hurdle) ~7%. Weights: equal 1/7. Cross-correlations ~0 (mean abs 0.05–0.15); only correlated pair ETH↔BTC ρ=0.54 at ~15% of book.

BAND_SYM_25 rule (STRATEGY.md:100-146): daily at 00:00 UTC rollover, share[n]=(realized balance + floating P&L)[n]/Σslots; re-split whole book to equal capital if any share > 0.25 (InpBandUp) or < (1/N)/1.75 = 0.0816 at N=7 (InpBandDownDiv=1.75); min 5 days between re-splits (InpBandMinGapDays=5, load-bearing, must be exact); S6's 3 legs summed into ONE slot (7 slots; per-leg iteration would give 9 slots, floor 0.0635, and misfire); floor computed at runtime from enabled-sleeve count, not hardcoded.

Harvest backstop: k=2.5 (InpHarvestK) re-seeds any slot >2.5× window-start seed; subsumed under the SYM band — 0/31 fires in Python; 5 fires in MT5 real-tick (RECONCILIATION.md table: 2020-08-07 BOOK_XAU 2.55×/share 0.232 true-backstop; 2021-01-07 S1_BTC 2.52×; 2022-04-20/29 and 2022-05-03 S5_JPY 2.53×/2.64×/2.52× — the 4 latter fired inside the 5-day band min-gap where BandTriggered() is muted but HarvestTriggered() is not).

H9 delta-resize (STRATEGY.md:149-163): same-sign sleeves ADD/REDUCE to the exact new equal-capital target at re-splits, refunding boundary spread+commission of a close+reopen; reversals close+reopen. Recon: 522 actions over 58 REBALs × 9 enabled sleeve-slots = 266 same-sign DELTA + 253 flat-target CLOSE + 3 reversal + 0 reopen-from-flat → 98.9% (266/269) of held-and-active resizes via delta path. Benefit +1.5pp, exists ONLY in MT5 — the validated 89.7% Python book close-and-reseeds and does not model it.

OFFICIAL HEADLINE = MT5 real-tick Strategy Tester, PortfolioV7.mq5, R10, IC Markets EU Raw €10k, 2020-01→2026-06 crash-inclusive (incl. Feb-2026 gold crash), reports _32–_37: CAGR 96.1%, Maximal eq-DD (€-worst) 20.9%, Relative eq-DD (%-worst crisis tail = COVID Mar-2020, €10.6k→€6.9k −34.9%) 35.6%, Sharpe 2.03, PF 1.67, min ML 190% (benign mid-2024; COVID ML rose 315%→1690%), history quality 84%, volume_rejects 0, losing quarters mark-to-market 3/24 (2020Q1 −21.8%, 2021Q3 −1.4%, 2022Q4 −6.3%), negative years 0/6.

€782,092 appears ONLY in docs/v7/DASHBOARD.html line 107 (hero equity; generator mt5/reconcile/v8/build_dashboard.py line 122) labeled '€10,000 → over 2020–2025 · 7-sleeve band core · single cross-margined account · R10 · MT5 real-tick, worst-mark accounting'. 78.2× over ~6.5y ≈ 95.5% CAGR — consistent with the full 2020-01→2026-06 MT5 R10 run despite the '2020–2025' subtitle. It is NOT in any of the six .md docs (those quote €532,230 for the Python R8 band book and €388,536 for V6).

Python band-book engine numbers (source-of-truth model, R8, IC 2020-25, close-and-reseed idealized): CAGR 89.7% (anchor 89.72%), MaxDD 15.70% bd close-mark / 19.44% tick-floating (honest, stricter — used throughout), Sharpe 2.58, final €532,230, 31 band triggers (0 harvest). Reproduces the G-BANDREBAL anchor to <1e-4 on all six fields. V6 reference: 80.3% / 17.31% bd / 20.74% tick / 2.35 / €388,536 / 3 harvest + 23 calendar re-splits.

MT5 R-frontier (PERFORMANCE.md:60-67; R | CAGR | Maximal | Relative | Sharpe | ML): 5 | 70.9 | 17.0 | 20.9 | 1.98 | 225; 8 | 86.5 | 21.2 | 30.4 | 1.97 | 210; 10 | 96.1 | 20.9 | 35.6 | 2.03 | 190; 12 | 99.8 | 21.4 | 39.2 | 2.00 | 200; 15 | 99.8 | 25.8 | 39.7 | 1.90 | 186; 20 | 99.6 | 26.3 | 39.3 | 1.85 | 187. Maximal (€-worst) is ~R-invariant; Relative (crisis %-worst) scales sharply with R. R12 = CAGR knee; R10 = live pick (Sharpe peak, lowest DD of high-R points).

V7 vs V6 (real-tick): V7@R10 96.1%/20.9% dominates V6@R8 84.5%/19.6% and V6@R10 88.4%/23.4% (both axes vs V6@R10); V6 blew to ~37% eq-DD at R15/R20 while V7 held ~26%. R5 anomaly: V7 (70.9%) is WORSE than V6 at R5 — down-band buying laggards hurts at low R during the Feb-2026 crash. DEMO.md: 'Do not run V7 below R8'.

Hard gates, 7/7 PASS (Python R8 battery, VALIDATION.md:62): CAGR>50% ✓, tick-DD<30% ✓, every-year-positive ✓, no-keystone (drop any sleeve keeps CAGR>50% AND tick-DD<30%) ✓, walk-forward+CPCV OOS (≥90% folds positive, median clears goal; OOS halves both +) ✓, MC p5 CAGR>40% ✓, MC P(DD>30%)<5% ✓. Separate softer GOAL: <20% tick-DD and >50% CAGR ('clears the 50% goal'). Dashboard gate set (DASHBOARD.html, different!): CAGR≥80% ✓ 96.1, MaxDD<30% ✓ 20.9, crisis tail ≤40% 'accepted' 35.6, Sharpe>2 nice-to-have ✓ 2.03, neg years 0 ✓ 0/6, neg qtrs ≤1 warn 3/24 → verdict '4/5 gates'.

Validation battery (6-tier): Tier 0 Python reproduction 6/6 fields <1e-4; Tier 1 headline + per-sleeve PF>1.2 + no-keystone knockouts (CAGR range 70.7% drop-gold to 93.1% drop-S6 which RAISES CAGR +3.39pp; tick-DD range 21.2–24.8% worst drop-ZC_EG; knockout down-floor rescaled to (1/6)/1.75=0.0952, up cap held at 0.25 — disclosed judgement call); Tier 2 walk-forward per year (2020 +116.5%, 2021 +98.2%, 2022 +52.4% worst, 2023 +87.4%, 2024 +93.5%, 2025 +124.4%; MaxDD 9.0–15.7%), CPCV purged 6 blocks k=2 5-bd embargo 15 folds (median 85.9%, range 63.7–112.5%, 15/15 positive), OOS halves DEV 2020-22 83.3% / HOLD 2023-25 96.4%, 1/N optimal OOS (eq-wt Sharpe 2.32 > max-Sharpe refit 1.89, min-var 2.34 reinvents 1/N with 0.0pp dispersion); Tier 3 band-width plateau (up 0.20→82.0, 0.22→79.5, 0.25→89.7, 0.28→90.6, 0.30→91.5; min 79.5% = 87% of peak, gate ≥75%; tick-DD breaches 20% at up=0.28) + rebalance-offset honest DD worst 19.44% (0/+21/+42d offsets); Tier 4 Monte Carlo 8,000 block-bootstrap histories blocks 20/63/120 (p5 CAGR 63.3%, median 89.4%, P(DD>30%) max 0.47%; 1.4–7.7% of paths had a losing 12-month window) + A2 joint-sleeve bootstrap (p5 53–57%, median 75.5%, P(DD>30%) 0.74%); Tier 5 diversification structural. NOTE: DSR, 'bootstrap breach' and 'pre-registered bars' are NOT in the six shipped docs — pre-registration lives in research/GBANDREBAL_RESULTS.md (the band ingredient's own pre-registered gate battery) covered by another agent.

Stress scenarios (INFORMATIONAL, set live R, not pass/fail; Python R8): gold flat from 2024 → 58.9% CAGR/16.0% DD; gold flat entire 6y → 57.5%/14.6%; worst ordering of 6 years (720 perms) 16.0–16.4% DD; tail-days ×1.5 → 23.3% DD (CAGR 49.5%); corr-spike ρ=0.8 → 35.1% DD (#1 hidden risk); ρ=1.0 → 38.5%; tail ×2 → 36.2% DD (CAGR 17.3%). Both real tails scale ~linearly with R. V6 same-run anchors: corr-0.8 38.7%, tail×2 42.3% (V6 doc quoted proxies 39%/44% — different-method footnote disclosed).

Reconciliation (RECONCILIATION.md): verdict `reconciled_with_notes`. Log portfolio_v7_decisions.csv 6919 rows 2020-01→2026-06; reference built READ-ONLY from gbandrebal/sim.run_generic + lock_v5.build_sleeves(R=10, core7sm) with MAG→BTC swap. Sleeve-m: 4561 rows, 85.7% exact <1e-3, capped/deterministic 292/300 byte-exact (S6 legs 210/210 at ±6 cap; S1_BTC/S1_ETH cap 1.2), direction agreement 99.4% (4482/4507), 650 residuals all attributed (warmup_boundary 267, us5_monday_exit 198 (21:00 UTC), zc_eg_zreversion 157 (sign agrees 95.9%, r=0.93), xau_night_boundary 17 (08:00/20:00), vol_estimate_noise 11), UNEXPLAINED = 0. Band: Python R10 27 triggers (11 up/16 dn) vs EA 57 (53 in 2020-25 + 4 in 2026H1; 31 up/26 dn); dead-zone (non-genuine) fires = 0; EA fires ~2× by design (realized slot equity + floating carryover). Artifacts mt5/reconcile/v7recon/recon.py + recon.json, runtime 1.02 min.

Retention ratio: at matched R8, MT5 real-tick retains ~96% of Python CAGR (86.5% vs 89.7%); V6 retained only ~65–73%. Explicitly 'observed, not guaranteed'. Note stale line: VALIDATION.md:14-15 still says 'MT5 real-tick historically lands ~65% of the Python CAGR'.

Trigger-count vocabulary (avoid confusion): Python R8 validated book = 31 band triggers/6y (~5/yr); Python R10 recon reference = 27 (16 down); EA R10 = 53 over 2020-25, 57 incl. 2026H1 (~8.8/yr; TRADE_CHARACTERISTICS says 5–12/yr, ~9/yr avg); DEMO monitoring band ~5–9 re-splits/yr; V6 comparison ~26 calendar+harvest events.

Per-sleeve trade profile (inherited from V6, confirmed not recomputed; TRADE_CHARACTERISTICS.md Part 1): BOOK_XAU 269 tr/yr, win 54%, PF 1.86, hold 24d, 81% long; BOOK_USTEC 55/65%/2.00/22d/100%; S5_JPY 15/38%/1.91/21d/100%; S1_ETH 6/69%/4.46/34d/100%; ZC_EG 80/67%/1.57/8d/47%; BTC_REP 8/60%/3.41/20d/100%; S6_OPEXUSD 36/59%/1.97/4d/34%. Portfolio ~468 trades/yr, win 58%, PF 1.96, 75% long, every sleeve PF>1.2. Knockout ΔCAGR: gold −19.06pp, USTEC −15.97, JPY −13.88, ZC_EG −13.37, ETH −10.39, BTC −3.86, S6 +3.39.

Account/cost model (STRATEGY.md:91-97): 7 sleeves share ONE real €10k hedging account as magic sub-accounts (magic base 360000); no per-sleeve liquidation, only account-level 50%-margin stop-out (never triggered, min ML 190% at R10). IC Raw costs: commission €3.25/side FX & metals; index & crypto commission-free; swap from central-bank policy-rate step model + broker markup; spread from actual tick-derived bid/ask.

Preset V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set non-default inputs (DEMO.md/STRATEGY.md): InpRisk 10.0, InpInitial 10000.0, InpMagicBase 360000, InpEqualWeight true, InpEnableS6 true, InpEnableBTC true, InpEnableFXTUJ/AU/EU false (legacy off), InpUS500=USTEC, InpBandUp 0.25, InpBandDownDiv 1.75, InpBandMinGapDays 5, InpHarvestK 2.5.

H8 rebalance science rationale (STRATEGY.md §6): re-split premium ~26pp of ~80% CAGR (quarterly vs never: +26.36pp CAGR / +7.63pp DD); all 600 block-shuffled re-runs keep positive premium; ordering contribution ~0pp (worst ≤ +5.7pp); conservative structural share 78%; premium is distributional (fat-tail concentration harvest), not temporal. T6 cadence frontier: BAND_SYM_25 +9.8pp CAGR / −1.6pp DD vs quarterly incumbent (15.70%/19.44% vs 17.31%/20.74%). No-rebalance counterfactual: ZC_EG reached 53% of book equity. 0.25 is 'the widest band on the plateau that still satisfies the <20% goal — a deployable center by constraint, not a tuned peak. Ship at up ≤ 0.25 strictly.'

DEMO monitoring fingerprints: sleeve P&L mix per table; ~468 trades/yr; every sleeve PF>1.2; S5_JPY must EARN swap; ZC_EG must take shorts; band cadence ~5–9/yr, meaningfully down-band-heavy; volume_rejects stays 0; ML>150% (the true survival signal, not DD%); crisis floating DD up to ~35–40% is EXPECTED at R10. Decision rules: after ≥3–4 months, if CAGR persistently <~50% or eqDD toward ~26%, investigate then step R toward 8 (never up); ML→150% step R to 8; sleeves co-moving = #1 hidden risk → step R to 8. Health file portfolio_v7_health.csv appended once per run at OnDeinit: version, risk, split_events, volume_rejects, closed_defers, final_equity, final_ML.

Doc-style conventions to replicate: (1) H1 'V7.0 <topic>' + bold single-paragraph definition naming code source of truth (EA + preset + Python engine paths in backticks) and cross-linking sibling docs with relative md links; (2) in-sample disclaimer near top AND bottom of every doc ('There is no post-2026 holdout — the live demo is the falsification test'); (3) pipe tables for every numeric claim, bold key figures, pp for percentage points, two-DD vocabulary (bd close-mark vs tick-floating 'honest'; MT5 Maximal €-worst vs Relative %-worst); (4) named honesty sections ('Honest caveats', 'Honest scope', 'Honest notes / divergences (none is a defect)', 'Engine honesty', 'observed, not guaranteed'); (5) VALIDATION scorecard table columns Tier|Test|Result|Gate|Verdict with ✅ PASS / ⏳ OPEN; sign-off as [x]/[ ] checkboxes split DONE vs OPEN; (6) RECONCILIATION leads with bold verdict token line 1, sections (A)/(B), numbered staging-review checks, ends 'Bottom line'; (7) TRADE_CHARACTERISTICS declares two measurement layers up front (Part 1 inherited/confirmed-not-recomputed, Part 2 new); (8) DEMO: dated 'Where we stand', numbered deployment steps, Watch|Expected fingerprint|Red flag table, numbered decision rules, 'Definition of done'; (9) artifacts/sources listed in italics at doc end; (10) disclosed judgement calls inline ('*Judgement call disclosed:*').
architecture: Single MT5 hedging account (€10k, IC Markets EU Raw, ESMA), EA PortfolioV7.mq5 (magic base 360000) attached to any M1 chart, symbol-driven, preset V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set. Seven economically independent sleeves as magic sub-accounts sharing one cross-margined account, equal capital 1/7 each ("band core"): BOOK_XAU (XAUUSD, Donchian breakout + overnight value-area, ~43% of P&L), BOOK_USTEC (USTEC via InpUS500=USTEC, 200-MA regime + Monday effect, ~20%), S5_JPY (USDJPY rate-divergence carry, trend-gated, ~10%), S1_ETH (ETHUSD momentum, ~9%), ZC_EG (EURGBP multi-window z-score mean-reversion, ~8%, the only long/short sleeve), S6_OPEXUSD (3-leg opex-week USD basket USDJPY/AUDUSD/NZDUSD ~1/21 each, ~4%), BTC_REP (BTCUSD financing-hurdle momentum with −20%/yr hurdle, ~7%). Signals are causal (data through prior daily close), inverse-vol sized (vol-target × R / annualised vol → clip at caps: S6 legs ±6, S1_BTC/S1_ETH 1.2). Leverage R=10 live (single mechanical knob; battery validated at R8). Concentration-band re-split BAND_SYM_25: daily 00:00-UTC share check, re-split whole book to 1/N when any slot share >0.25 or <(1/N)/1.75 (0.0816 @ N=7), ≥5-day min gap; S6's 3 legs aggregated to ONE slot (7-slot test); k=2.5 harvest backstop retained (fires only inside the band min-gap or sub-0.25 over-compounding). At re-splits, H9 delta-resize: same-sign positions ADD/REDUCE to the new target (98.9% of held-and-active resizes), reversals close+reopen. No market-timing overlay; the re-split is the only active portfolio action. Conversion symbols EURUSD, EURJPY required in Market Watch. Logs: portfolio_v7_decisions.csv (OPEN/ADD/REDUCE/FLIP/CLOSE/REBAL/BAND/HARVEST rows), portfolio_v7_health.csv, heartbeat/state csv for restart recovery. Validation twin: Python band engine mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/sim.run_generic (= v51_bandharvest._run_window), idealized close-and-reseed, no delta-resize.
how_to_run: MT5 live-config reproduction: compile mt5/ea/PortfolioV7.mq5 in MetaEditor (F7), attach to any M1 chart or Strategy Tester on a hedging IC Markets EU Raw account, load mt5/presets/V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set, enable AutoTrading. Expected: reproduces the real-tick headline 96.1% CAGR / 20.9% Maximal eq-DD / 35.6% Relative / Sharpe 2.03 / ML 190% over 2020-01→2026-06 (R-frontier reports _32–_37). Startup journal must show 'SLOT-EQUAL over 7 slots (core 5, S6=1, BTC=1); slotW=0.1429' and 7 non-zero slots ≈0.1429 (3 S6 legs ≈0.0476 each).

Python validation battery: run mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier12.py (Tiers 1-2, single ~8.78-min synchronous run → tier12.json with 9/9 sub-gates, tier12_pass=true) and tier4.py (Tier 4 MC, ~3.0-min → tier4.json 'tier4=PASS'). Expected anchor: 89.72% CAGR / 15.70% bd-DD / 19.44% tick-DD / Sharpe 2.58 / final €532,230 / 31 band triggers, matched <1e-4 on all six fields.

Band-ingredient battery (CPCV/OOS-halves/plateau): mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/ — robustness.json holds the cited results; engine is READ-ONLY sim.run_generic = v51_bandharvest._run_window. Book construction: run_generic(7 sleeves, [2020-01,2026-01], up=0.25, down=W7/1.75, k=2.5).

EA↔Python reconciliation: mt5/reconcile/v7recon/recon.py (runtime 1.02 min → recon.json), diffing the tester log portfolio_v7_decisions.csv (6919 rows) against the R10 Python reference (gbandrebal/sim.run_generic + lock_v5.build_sleeves(R=10, core7sm) with MAG→BTC swap). Expected verdict: reconciled_with_notes, 0 unexplained mismatches, 0 dead-zone band fires.

Demo deployment (DEMO.md exact steps): open IC Markets EU demo — Raw, EUR, hedging, ~€10,000, server family ICMarketsEU-MT5; Market Watch symbols XAUUSD, USTEC, USDJPY, ETHUSD, EURGBP, BTCUSD + S6 legs USDJPY/AUDUSD/NZDUSD + conversion EURUSD, EURJPY; compile EA; attach + load preset; verify decisions log shows USTEC trading (label may read BOOK_US5).
official_numbers: The headline quintet CAGR 96.1% / MaxDD 20.9% / Sharpe 2.03 / crisis tail 35.6% is the MT5 real-tick Strategy Tester run of PortfolioV7.mq5 at R10, IC Markets EU Raw €10k, 2020-01→2026-06 crash-inclusive (R-frontier reports _32–_37): 20.9% is MT5's "Maximal" drawdown (€-worst, late on a large account) and 35.6% is MT5's "Relative" drawdown (%-worst, the Mar-2020 COVID event, €10.6k→€6.9k), both reported first-class as different events. Also from that run: PF 1.67, min ML 190%, history quality 84%, volume_rejects 0, losing quarters 3/24 (worst −21.8%), negative years 0/6. €782,092 is the final equity of that same MT5 real-tick R10 run, but it appears ONLY in DASHBOARD.html (hero, "MT5 real-tick, worst-mark accounting"; generated by mt5/reconcile/v8/build_dashboard.py) — it is in none of the six shipped .md files. The Python band-book close-mark engine (validation source of truth, R8, IC 2020-25, close-and-reseed) produced 89.7% CAGR / 15.70% bd close-mark DD / 19.44% tick-floating DD / Sharpe 2.58 / final €532,230 / 31 band triggers, and it is on THIS run that the 7/7 hard-gate battery passed. No "1m worst-mark" engine is named in the six .md docs — the worst-mark label exists only in the dashboard; the .md two-mark vocabulary is bd close-mark vs tick-floating ("honest"). Matched-R8 retention MT5/Python = ~96% (86.5/89.7), vs V6's ~65–73%.
pitfalls: VALIDATION.md is internally stale: its header (lines 13-16) and sign-off (lines 232-243) still list the MT5 real-tick R-sweep and the PortfolioV7.mq5↔Python reconciliation as OPEN ('Not cleared for live demo yet') and quote the old ~65% retention, while STRATEGY.md, PERFORMANCE.md, RECONCILIATION.md and DEMO.md declare both DONE (verdict reconciled_with_notes) and quote ~96% retention. VALIDATION.md scorecard Tier 0 row also says the EA is 'staged, not compiled/reconciled'. Trust the later four docs for status.
Never run V7 below R8: at R5 the band is WORSE than V6 (down-band buying laggards hurt during the Feb-2026 crash). The band's edge is leverage-dependent, earned at the high-R end.

Ship the band strictly at up ≤ 0.25: CAGR keeps rising toward wider bands but tick-DD breaches the 20% goal at up=0.28. Treat any live drift wider as a goal violation, not an optimization. Only 0.56pp headroom on the binding 19.44% tick-DD.
The 5-day min gap is load-bearing — 'in every validated run, and must be implemented exactly, not approximated'.

S6 aggregation trap: the 3 S6 legs MUST be summed into one slot before the share test (7 slots, floor 0.0816). Per-leg iteration gives 9 slots, floor 0.0635, and misfires the down-band. The floor must be computed at runtime from the enabled-sleeve count, never hardcoded (knockout runs rescale it, e.g. (1/6)/1.75=0.0952).

The DD-halving is a sizing-mechanism product (slot-equity sizing × re-split, H8 M2×M4), not a market anomaly: under fixed-notional sizing rebalancing RAISES DD by re-grossing. Changing the sizing anchor voids the benefit.
Do not compare 96.1% (MT5, R10) with 89.7% (Python, R8) directly — different leverage; the honest comparison is matched-R8 retention (~96%, observed not guaranteed).
The +1.5pp delta-resize benefit exists only in MT5; the validated Python book does not model it. Day-after-re-split slot shares are the one legitimate EA-vs-Python divergence (floating carryover).
EA band-trigger count ≈2× the idealized Python count BY DESIGN (53 vs 27 over 2020-25); a matching count is not a health criterion — genuineness (0 dead-zone fires) is.

Earlier 'negative quarters = 0' figures in research docs are for the Python close-mark C2cfg (a different, unlevered config); the honest R10 real-tick count is 3/24, worst −21.8% (COVID). Also frame 'positive every year' carefully: 1.4–7.7% of bootstrap paths had a losing 12-month window.
Band-width sensitivity is real (family CAGR spread 79.3–89.7%); it is a weaker robustness guarantee than V6's calendar-offset grid.

Tail risks scale ~linearly with R: corr-spike ρ=0.8 → ~35% DD and tail×2 → ~36% at R8, larger at R10; no probability assignable (corr spike never occurred in-sample). This is why R stopped at 10, below the R12 knee. R8 (86.5%/21.2%, ML 210%) is the pre-validated fallback.
Gold is ~43% of P&L — the single concentration to monitor; book survives without it (drop-gold 70.7% CAGR, gold-flat 57.5%) but a persistent gold-regime failure is the largest degradation path.
Everything is in-sample (IC 2020-25 + 2026-H1 real-tick); no post-2026 holdout exists — the live demo is the falsification test.

Terminology traps: the EA decisions log may label the equity sleeve BOOK_US5 but it trades USTEC (InpUS500=USTEC); the EA startup banner still prints 'PortfolioV5:'; the BAND floor logs as 0.08 (2 dp) for true 0.0816; DASHBOARD.html gate set (CAGR≥80, tail≤40 accepted, 4/5 verdict) differs from the Python battery's 7 hard gates — do not conflate.

DSR, bootstrap-breach and pre-registered-bar specifics are NOT in the six shipped docs; the pre-registered battery for the band ingredient is docs/v7/research/GBANDREBAL_RESULTS.md (another agent's scope).
V7.1 (overlay ring, C2cfg, +5.9pp joint) is explicitly a separate future book with its own battery — not part of V7.0.
DASHBOARD.html is generated by mt5/reconcile/v8/build_dashboard.py — v8 tooling already exists in the repo; check that directory before assuming v7 is the latest engine work.
